page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96-lssd is a high-compute server instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance includes 360 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core. The processor is equipped with a 504 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It features advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions for accelerated matrix and vector operations.

This server configuration includes 6167 GB of local NVMe SSD storage distributed across 16 local SSDs. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware. The high-capacity local storage is directly attached, offering low-latency data access suitable for intensive read and write operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the c4-standard-96-lssd demonstrates top-tier performance. It achieves top 10% placement in Passmark database operations, Redis SET operations reaching 171.76 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 150.43 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds in the top 10% at 7717.61 GB/sec and large block uncached read speeds at 356.38 GB/sec. CPU-bound tasks such as software compilation, ray tracing, and cryptography also rank in the top 10%. LLM inference benchmarks show strong prompt processing capabilities, though text generation speeds for larger models remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the integration of 16 local NVMe SSDs alongside 96 dedicated vCPUs provides high resource density, eliminating the need for external block storage for high-throughput workloads. This makes the instance highly suitable for large-scale database deployments, high-performance computing, software build pipelines, and CPU-based machine learning or inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96-lssd is a high-compute server configuration powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 360 GB of RAM. This x86_64 architecture instance features a massive 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. It comes bundled with 6167 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 16 drives, but lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations at 171.76 million operations per second, and static web serving. Memory read bandwidth is exceptionally high, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. This resource-dense profile offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy, compute-intensive workloads. It is highly recommended for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96-lssd is a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon server with 360 GB RAM and 6167 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database, memory bandwidth, and compute performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96-lssd features 96 vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and 6167 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A high-compute, storage-dense server featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and over six terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors with AMX and AVX-512 support.
360 GB of system memory paired with a large 504 MB L3 cache.
6167 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across 16 drives.
Top-tier database performance, achieving 171.76 million Redis SET operations per second.
Excellent fit for large-scale databases, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
